title: Bridge Essay: Predicting the Sensory Consequences of Self-Generated Actions: Pre-Supplementary

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**Predicting the Sensory Consequences of Self-Generated Actions: Pre-Supplementary Motor Area as Supra-Modal Hub in the Sense of Agency Experience.**. Brain Sci.. PMID: 33171715. DOI: 10.3390/brainsci10110825. Sense of agency refers to the feeling that one'sself-generated action caused an external environment event. In aprevious study, we suggested that the supplementary motor area (SMA), in its anterior portion (pre-SMA), is akey structure for attributing the sense of agency for the visual consequences of self-generated movements. However, real-life actions can lead to outcomes in different sensory modalities, raising the question of whether SMA represents asupra-modal hub for the sense of agency
